volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: The Rope (Section 4.4.1120 - Section 4.4.1177)

sections.label: Section 4.4.1120
sections.original: ut iubeas hunc reddere illis ; ob eam si quid postulat
sections.translation: to order him to give up that casket to these young women ; if for it he asks any reward for himself , it shall be paid ; whatever else is there besides , let him keep for himself .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1122
sections.original: Nunc demum istuc dicis , quoniam ius meum esse intellegis :
sections.translation: Now at last you say that , because you are aware it is my right ; just now you were asking to go halves .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1123b
sections.original: Immo etiam nunc peto .
sections.translation: Aye , and even still I ask it .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1124
sections.original: Vidi petere miluom , etiam cum nihil auferret tamen .
sections.translation: I ’ ve seen a kite making a swoop , even when he got nothing at all however .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1125
sections.original: Non ego te comprimere possum sine malo ?
sections.translation: Can ’ t I shut your mouth without a drubbing ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1125b
sections.original: Si istic tacet ,
sections.translation: If that fellow is silent , I ’ ll be silent ; if he talks , allow me to talk in my own behalf .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1127
sections.original: Cedo modó mihi vidulum istum , Gripe .
sections.translation: Please now give me this wallet , Gripus .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1127b
sections.original: Concedam tibi ,
sections.translation: I ’ ll trust it to you ; but for you to return it me , if there are none of those things in it .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1128b
sections.original: Reddetur .
sections.translation: It shall be returned .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1128c
sections.original: Tene .
sections.translation: Take it .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1129
sections.original: Audi nunciam , Palaestra atque Ampelisca , hoc quod loquor .
sections.translation: Now then listen , Palaestra and Ampelisca , to this which I say :

sections.label: Section 4.4.1130
sections.original: estne hic vidulus úbi cistellam tuam inesse aiebas ?
sections.translation: is this the wallet , in which this Procurer said that your casket was ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1130b
sections.original: Is est .
sections.translation: It is the same .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1131
sections.original: Perii hercle ego miser , uti , prius quam plane aspexit , ilico
sections.translation: Troth , to my sorrow , I ’ m undone ; how on the instant , before she well saw it , she said that it was it .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1132b
sections.original: Faciam ego hanc rem ex procliva pla nam tibi .
sections.translation: I ’ ll make this matter plain to you , instead of difficult . There ought to be a casket of wicker-work there in that wallet ; whatever is in there I ’ ll state by name ; don ’ t you

sections.label: Section 4.4.1135
sections.original: nullum ostenderis ; si falsa dicam , frustra dixero ,
sections.translation: show me anything . If I say wrong , I shall then have said this to no purpose ; then you shall keep these things , whatever is in there for yourselves . But if the truth , then I entreat you that what is my own may be restored to me .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1137b
sections.original: Placet .
sections.translation: I agree ; you ask for bare justice only , in my way of thinking , at least .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1138b
sections.original: Át meo hercle iniuriam .
sections.translation: But , I ’ faith , in mine , for extreme injustice ; what if she is a witch or a sorceress ,

sections.label: Section 4.4.1140
sections.original: quidquid insit , vera dicet ? anne habebit hariola ?
sections.translation: and shall mention exactly everything that ’ s in it . Is a sorceress to have it ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1141
sections.original: Non feret , nisi vera dicet : nequiquam hariolabitur .
sections.translation: She shan ’ t get it , unless she tells the truth ; in vain will she [*] be conjuring . Unloose the wallet , then ( giving it to GRIPUS ) , that as soon as possible I may know what is the truth .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1143
sections.original: Hoc habet , solutust .
sections.translation: Take it [*] , it ’ s unfastened . ( DAEMONES takes out the casket .) Alas , I ’ m undone ; I see the casket .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1143b
sections.original: Aperi . video cistellam . haecinest ?
sections.translation: Is this it ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1144
sections.original: Istaec est . o mei parentes , hic vos conclusos gero ,
sections.translation: That is it . O my parents , here do I keep you locked up ;

sections.label: Section 4.4.1145
sections.original: huc opesque spesque vestrum cognoscendum condidi .
sections.translation: here have I enclosed both my wealth and my hopes of recognizing you .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1146
sections.original: Tum tibi hercle deos iratos esse oportet , quisquis es ,
sections.translation: Then , by my faith , the Gods must be enraged with you , whoever you are , who fasten up your parents in so narrow a compass .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1148
sections.original: Gripe , accede huc ; tua res agitur . tu , puella , istinc procul
sections.translation: Gripus , come hither , your cause is being tried . ( To PALAESTRA .) Do you , young woman , away at a distance there say what ’ s in it , and of what appearance ; mention them all .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1150
sections.original: si hercle tantillum peccassis , quod posterius postules
sections.translation: By my troth , if you make ever so slight a mistake , even if afterwards you wish , madam , to correct yourself , you ’ ll be making a great mistake .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1152
sections.original: Ius bonum oras .
sections.translation: You demand what ’ s real justice .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1152b
sections.original: Edepol haud te órat , nam tu iniuriu'siniurius es .
sections.translation: By my troth , then , he doesn ’ t demand yourself ; for you are the opposite of justice .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1153
sections.original: Loquere nunciam , puella . Gripe , animum advorte ac tace .
sections.translation: Now then , say on , young woman . Gripus , give attention and hold your tongue .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1154
sections.original: Sunt crepundia .
sections.translation: There are some trinkets .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1154b
sections.original: Ecca video .
sections.translation: See , here they are , I espy them .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1154c
sections.original: Perii in primo proelio .
sections.translation: In the first onset I am worsted ;

sections.label: Section 4.4.1155
sections.original: mane , ne ostenderis .
sections.translation: ( takes hold of the arm of DAEMONES ) hold , don ’ t be showing .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1155b
sections.original: Qua facie sunt ? responde ex ordine .
sections.translation: Of what description are they ? Answer in their order .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1156
sections.original: Ensiculust aureolus primum litteratus .
sections.translation: In the first place , there ’ s a little sword of gold , with an inscription .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1156b
sections.original: Dice dum ,
sections.translation: Just tell me , what the characters are upon that little sword .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1157b
sections.original: Mei nomen patris .
sections.translation: The name of my father . Next , on the other side , there ’ s a little two-edged axe , of gold likewise , with an inscription : there on the axe is the name of my mother .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1159b
sections.original: Mane .
sections.translation: Stay ;

sections.label: Section 4.4.1160
sections.original: dic , in ensiculo quid nomen est paternum ?
sections.translation: tell me , what ’ s the name of your father upon the little sword ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1160b
sections.original: Daemones .
sections.translation: Daemones .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1161
sections.original: Di immortales , ubi loci sunt spes meae ?
sections.translation: Immortal Gods ! where in the world are my hopes ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1161b
sections.original: Immo edepol meae ?
sections.translation: Aye , by my troth , and where are mine ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1162
sections.original: Pergite , opsecro , continuo .
sections.translation: Do proceed forthwith , I entreat you .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1162b
sections.original: Placide , aut i in malam crucem .
sections.translation: Cautiously , or else ( aside ) away to utter perdition .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1163
sections.original: Loquere matris nomen hic quid in securicula siet .
sections.translation: Say , what ’ s the name of your mother , here upon the little axe ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1164
sections.original: Daedalis .
sections.translation: Daedalis .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1164b
sections.original: Di me servatum cupiunt .
sections.translation: The Gods will that I should be preserved .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1164c
sections.original: At me perditum .
sections.translation: But that I should be ruined .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1165
sections.original: Filiam meam esse hánc oportet , Gripe .
sections.translation: This must be my own daughter , Gripus .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1165b
sections.original: Sit per me quidem .
sections.translation: She may be for me , indeed . ( To TRACHALIO .) May all the Gods confound you who this day saw me with your eyes , and myself as well for a blockhead , who didn ’ t look about a hundred times first to see that no one was watching me , before I drew the net out of the water .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1169
sections.original: Post sicilicula argenteola et duae conexae maniculae et
sections.translation: Next , there ’ s a little knife of silver , and two little hands linked together , and

sections.label: Section 4.4.1170
sections.original: sucula .
sections.translation: then a little sow .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1170b
sections.original: Quin tu i díerecta , cum sucula et cum porculis .
sections.translation: Nay , then , go and be hanged , you with your little sow and with your little pigs .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1171
sections.original: Et bulla aurea est , pater quam dedit mi natali die .
sections.translation: There ’ s also a golden drop [*] , which my father presented to me upon my birthday .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1172
sections.original: Ea est profecto . contineri quin complectar non queo .
sections.translation: Undoubtedly there is ; but I cannot restrain myself any longer from embracing you . My daughter , blessings on you ; I am that father who begot you ; I am Daemones , and see , your mother Daedalis is in the house here ( pointing to his cottage ) .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1175
sections.original: Salve , mi pater insperate .
sections.translation: Blessings on you , my unlooked-for father .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1175b
sections.original: Salve . ut te amplector libens .
sections.translation: Blessings on you ; how joyously do I embrace you .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1176
sections.original: Volup est cum istuc ex pietate vestra vobis contigit .
sections.translation: ’ Tis a pleasure to me , inasmuch as this falls to your lot from your feelings of affection .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1177
sections.original: Cape dum , hunc si potes fer intro vidulum , age , Trachalio .
sections.translation: Come then , Trachalio , if you can , bring that wallet into the house .
